---
title: "On Minimalistic Maps: Mapping Population Density in the Philippines"
description: "We take a break and try to plot some cool minimalistic plots of population density in the Philippines."
author:
  - name: TJ Palanca
    url: https://www.twitter.com/tjpalanca
date: 05-01-2017
output:
  distill::distill_article:
    self_contained: false
    toc: true
preview: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/05-bicol.png
categories:
  - maps
  - dataviz
  - philippines
repository_url: https://github.com/tjpalanca/population-lines
---

## Minimalistic maps

Taking the idea from the [Population Lines Print](http://spatial.ly/2014/08/population-lines/) by Spatial.ly / Dr James Cheshire, we plot local population density maps for the Philippines in the same style. The data is taken from the [NASA Socioeconomic Data and Applications Center (SEDAC)](http://sedac.ciesin.columbia.edu/data/set/gpw-v4-population-density-adjusted-to-2015-unwpp-country-totals/data-download).

## Philippine population density maps

Fortunately, the process was relatively straightforward and it was not difficult to produce the same cool faux-3D population peak effect with a bit of data wrangling and good old `ggplot2`. Here are some I've produced for key Philippine metropolitan areas and my home region Bicol.

<div class="l-body-outset uk-card uk-card-body uk-card-default" style="margin-bottom: 30px;">
<ul uk-tab>
<li><a href="#">Philippines</a></li>
<li><a href="#">Greater Manila</a></li>
<li><a href="#">Cebu</a></li>
<li><a href="#">Davao</a></li>
<li><a href="#">Bicol Region</a></li>
</ul>
<ul class="uk-switcher uk-margin">
<li>
<a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/01-philippines.png">
<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/01-philippines.png"/>
</a>
</li>
<li>
<a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/02-greater-manila-area.png">
<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/02-greater-manila-area.png"/>
</a>
</li>
<li>
<a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/03-cebu.png">
<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/03-cebu.png"/>
</a>
</li>
<li>
<a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/04-davao.png">
<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/04-davao.png"/>
</a>
</li>
<li>
<a href="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/05-bicol.png">
<img src="https://raw.githubusercontent.com/tjpalanca/population-lines/master/out/05-bicol.png"/>
</a>
</li>
</ul>
</div>

## Reproducibility 

Here are the notebooks containing the code, results, and commentary behind the post. You may download the resulting code and run it yourself, provided:

* You have to download the population density maps on your own access rights, and
* You agree that this is provided as-is and with no warranty.
  
You may also view the GitHub Repository [here](https://github.com/tjpalanca/population-lines) for the complete code and analysis that went into this post.
